title: laggera viscosa
intro: Laggera viscosa, also known as West African spearleaf, is a plant species native to tropical Africa, characterized by its distinctive spear-shaped leaves and sticky sap. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Laggera viscosa include reducing fever, relieving pain and swelling, and aiding in wound healing. Laggera viscosa is commonly prepared as an infusion or decoction, and can also be made into a paste or ointment for topical application, and its essential oil has also been used in traditional medicine.
